data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "For any finite pool of k candidates, the harness-filtered majority vote can NEVER strictly beat self-consistency on the SAME pool when the critic operates on candidate-answer strings alone (no privileged answer-key or gold feedback), because the critic's error is confounded with the candidate's error: the candidates most likely to produce a wrong-majority pool are exactly the ones for which the critic is also most likely to err, so erosion always cancels rescue in expectation.", "mechanism": "Under a model where the critic M_c is a peer of the candidate generator M_g with correlated errors (both draw from the same model family / training data), there exists a shared latent difficulty variable D such that high-D items simultaneously increase candidate base error AND critic false-flag rate. On high-D items (where rescue is needed because the majority is fragile), critic precision collapses (as observed: precision=0.125 on hard-set). On low-D items (where precision is higher), the majority is already robust so rescue is unnecessary. This creates an anti-correlation between the value of intervention and the quality of intervention, making strict harness > SC impossible in expectation with a single peer critic. The only escape is to decorrelate critic error from candidate error, e.g., by using a critic from a different model family with orthogonal failure modes, which is outside the stated single-provider setup.", "scope": {"covers": ["k=5 single-provider studies where the critic is the same model family as the candidate generator", "critic sees only candidate answers, no gold label", "binary QA"]}, "excludes": ["cross-family critic (e.g., GPT-class critic on Llama-class candidates)", "critic with access to external verification tools (code execution, retrieval)", "human-in-the-loop critic"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "The peer-critic arm achieves harness accuracy strictly greater than SC accuracy (binomial p < 0.05, one-sided) in ANY difficulty bin or k condition, which would refute the impossibility claim for the single-peer-critic regime", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "The cross-family critic arm achieves harness accuracy <= SC accuracy in the mid-zone bin despite having measured precision > 0.5 and recall > 0.5, which would refute the claim that decorrelation is the necessary escape", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This is the skeptical atypical candidate: it asserts the refuted 133fcf5d was not wrong about the regime but wrong about the possibility—strict dominance is impossible within a single provider's model family because of error correlation. The neighbourhood's 30e483eb (adaptive confidence) is a partial escape but only if the adaptation can break the correlation, which a peer model cannot reliably do. The experiment includes a cross-family critic arm as a contrast: if the cross-family arm wins in the mid-zone while the peer arm does not, the claim is confirmed. Token budget: ~225k."}
